nodes.name: plugin-database.ts
nodes.summary: Provides SQL validation, namespace derivation, migration execution, and a sandboxed database service for plugins, enforcing strict restrictions on DDL/DML to prevent plugins from accessing unauthorized data.

nodes.name: derivePluginDatabaseNamespace
nodes.summary: Derives a safe, deterministic database namespace string for a plugin by combining a SHA-256 hash prefix with a slugified human-readable name.

nodes.name: validatePluginMigrationStatement
nodes.summary: Validates a single SQL migration statement to ensure it only touches the plugin's own namespace and does not use banned SQL constructs.

nodes.name: validatePluginRuntimeQuery
nodes.summary: Validates a runtime SELECT query from a plugin, ensuring it only reads from its own namespace or explicitly allowed core tables.

nodes.name: validatePluginRuntimeExecute
nodes.summary: Validates a runtime INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE statement from a plugin, restricting modifications to the plugin's own namespace tables.

nodes.name: pluginDatabaseService
nodes.summary: Factory that creates a fully scoped database service for a plugin, exposing migration running, query execution, and data access all confined to the plugin's namespace.

nodes.name: plugin-dev-watcher.ts
nodes.summary: Implements a filesystem watcher for local plugin development, detecting source changes and triggering automatic plugin reloads during development.

nodes.name: resolvePluginWatchTargets
nodes.summary: Resolves the list of filesystem paths that should be watched for a plugin, including source directories and manifest files.

nodes.name: createPluginDevWatcher
nodes.summary: Creates and manages a chokidar-based file watcher for local plugin development, debouncing changes and dispatching reload events.

nodes.name: plugin-environment-driver.ts
nodes.summary: Provides resolution and lifecycle management utilities for plugin environment drivers and sandbox providers, including validation, probing, and workspace command execution.

nodes.name: resolvePluginEnvironmentDriver
nodes.summary: Resolves the appropriate environment driver for a plugin based on its configuration and registered drivers.

nodes.name: validatePluginEnvironmentDriverConfig
nodes.summary: Validates the configuration object for a plugin environment driver, reporting structured errors for misconfigured fields.

nodes.name: executePluginEnvironmentCommand
nodes.summary: Executes a shell command within a plugin's managed environment workspace via the environment driver.

nodes.name: plugin-event-bus.ts
nodes.summary: Implements a typed publish-subscribe event bus for plugin system events, supporting pattern-based topic matching and per-subscriber filtering.

nodes.name: createPluginEventBus
nodes.summary: Factory that creates a plugin event bus instance with subscribe, publish, and unsubscribe capabilities including wildcard pattern matching.

nodes.name: plugin-host-service-cleanup.ts
nodes.summary: Provides a cleanup coordinator for plugin host services, managing ordered teardown of registered disposable resources on plugin unload.

nodes.name: plugin-host-services.ts
nodes.summary: Implements the comprehensive host services API exposed to plugins at runtime, including HTTP fetch, filesystem access, logging, state management, and tool invocation, all with strict capability enforcement.
nodes.languageNotes: The largest service file at 2151 lines; buildHostServices is the central factory assembling all host-facing plugin APIs.

nodes.name: buildHostServices
nodes.summary: Master factory that assembles all host service capabilities (HTTP, storage, logging, tools, events, secrets) into a single object passed to plugin sandboxes.

nodes.name: flushPluginLogBuffer
nodes.summary: Flushes buffered plugin log entries to persistent storage, batching inserts to reduce database write pressure.

nodes.name: plugin-job-coordinator.ts
nodes.summary: Coordinates concurrent plugin job execution by managing worker assignment, concurrency limits, and job lifecycle transitions across multiple plugin workers.

nodes.name: createPluginJobCoordinator
nodes.summary: Creates the job coordinator that routes incoming jobs to available workers, enforces concurrency limits, and handles job queuing and cancellation.

nodes.name: plugin-job-scheduler.ts
nodes.summary: Implements a cron-based job scheduler for plugins, managing scheduled trigger registration, firing, and lifecycle tied to plugin enable/disable state.

nodes.name: createPluginJobScheduler
nodes.summary: Creates the plugin job scheduler, registering cron schedules from plugin manifests and dispatching jobs at the configured intervals.

nodes.name: plugin-job-store.ts
nodes.summary: Provides a persistent store for plugin job records including creation, status updates, result storage, and querying of job history.

nodes.name: pluginJobStore
nodes.summary: Factory that creates the plugin job store with CRUD operations for job records, leveraging the database layer for persistence.

nodes.name: plugin-lifecycle.ts
nodes.summary: Manages the complete lifecycle state machine for plugins (loading, installing, enabling, disabling, uninstalling), coordinating transitions and emitting lifecycle events.

nodes.name: pluginLifecycleManager
nodes.summary: Creates the plugin lifecycle manager that orchestrates all state transitions, calling install, enable, disable, and uninstall hooks in the correct order.

nodes.name: plugin-loader.ts
nodes.summary: Central plugin loading engine that discovers, reads, validates, and prepares plugin packages for installation, handling both npm-published and local plugins.
nodes.languageNotes: Second largest service file at 2066 lines; pluginLoader is the primary factory coordinating all loading concerns.

nodes.name: pluginLoader
nodes.summary: Core plugin loader factory that discovers plugin packages, reads manifests, validates compatibility, and prepares plugin descriptors for the registry.

nodes.name: resolveWorkerEntrypoint
nodes.summary: Resolves the JavaScript entrypoint file for a plugin worker process, validating its existence and format.

nodes.name: plugin-local-folders.ts
nodes.summary: Implements sandboxed local folder access for plugins, providing validated read/write/list/delete operations restricted to declared folder boundaries.

nodes.name: inspectPluginLocalFolder
nodes.summary: Performs a full inspection of a plugin's local folder, gathering metadata, validating configuration, and returning a structured report.

nodes.name: writePluginLocalFolderTextAtomic
nodes.summary: Atomically writes text content to a file within a plugin's declared local folder, using a temp-file rename pattern to prevent partial writes.

nodes.name: listPluginLocalFolderEntries
nodes.summary: Lists entries in a plugin's local folder with pagination, filtering, and stat collection, respecting maxEntries limits.

nodes.name: plugin-log-retention.ts
nodes.summary: Implements log retention management for plugins, periodically pruning old log records to prevent unbounded database growth.

nodes.name: plugin-managed-agents.ts
nodes.summary: Manages plugin-declared managed agents, handling CRUD synchronization between plugin manifests and the database agent records with adapter preference resolution.

nodes.name: pluginManagedAgentService
nodes.summary: Creates the managed agent service that syncs plugin-declared agents to the database, applying defaults and managing lifecycle upserts and deletions.

nodes.name: plugin-managed-routines.ts
nodes.summary: Manages plugin-declared scheduled routines, syncing manifest-defined routines to the database and handling upsert/delete lifecycle for routine records.

nodes.name: pluginManagedRoutineService
nodes.summary: Creates the managed routine service that synchronizes plugin-declared routines with the database, computing trigger schedules and handling full lifecycle management.

nodes.name: plugin-managed-skills.ts
nodes.summary: Manages plugin-declared skills, generating skill package files from manifest declarations and synchronizing skill metadata with the database.

nodes.name: pluginManagedSkillService
nodes.summary: Creates the managed skill service that syncs plugin-declared skills to the database, building YAML package files and managing upsert/delete lifecycle.

nodes.name: plugin-manifest-validator.ts
nodes.summary: Validates plugin manifest files against the schema, reporting structured validation errors for malformed or missing manifest fields.

nodes.name: pluginManifestValidator
nodes.summary: Creates a manifest validator instance that checks plugin manifests and returns typed validation results.

nodes.name: plugin-registry.ts
nodes.summary: Maintains the central in-memory and database registry of installed plugins, tracking plugin metadata, status, configuration, and providing lookup APIs.

nodes.name: pluginRegistryService
nodes.summary: Creates the plugin registry service with full CRUD over plugin records, reactive state tracking, and query capabilities for the plugin management system.

nodes.name: plugin-runtime-sandbox.ts
nodes.summary: Implements a Node.js VM-based sandbox for loading and executing plugin modules with capability-scoped APIs and strict module isolation.

nodes.name: PluginSandboxError
nodes.summary: Custom error class representing failures that occur within the plugin sandbox execution environment.

nodes.name: loadPluginModuleInSandbox
nodes.summary: Loads a plugin module into a Node.js VM sandbox context, injecting scoped host APIs and intercepting require calls to enforce module access policies.

nodes.name: plugin-secrets-handler.ts
nodes.summary: Handles resolution and injection of secret references declared in plugin configurations, with rate limiting and access control enforcement.

nodes.name: createPluginSecretsHandler
nodes.summary: Creates a secrets handler for a plugin that resolves secret references from the store with rate limiting to prevent secret enumeration attacks.

nodes.name: extractSecretRefPathsFromConfig
nodes.summary: Recursively extracts all secret reference paths from a plugin configuration object for validation and resolution.

nodes.name: plugin-state-store.ts
nodes.summary: Provides a scoped key-value state store for plugins, persisting plugin-specific state to the database with namespace isolation per plugin.

nodes.name: pluginStateStore
nodes.summary: Creates a namespaced state store for a plugin with get, set, delete, and list operations backed by database persistence.

nodes.name: plugin-stream-bus.ts
nodes.summary: Implements a lightweight streaming message bus for plugin-to-host and host-to-plugin data streams, supporting multiplexed channels.

nodes.name: createPluginStreamBus
nodes.summary: Creates the stream bus instance that manages multiplexed streams keyed by plugin and channel identifier.

nodes.name: plugin-tool-dispatcher.ts
nodes.summary: Dispatches tool invocations from plugin workers to registered tool handlers, managing capability validation, timeouts, and result serialization.

nodes.name: createPluginToolDispatcher
nodes.summary: Creates the tool dispatcher that routes plugin tool calls to the correct handler, enforcing capability checks and handling streaming responses.

nodes.name: plugin-tool-registry.ts
nodes.summary: Maintains the registry of tools contributed by plugins, handling tool registration, deregistration, conflict detection, and namespace-qualified lookups.

nodes.name: createPluginToolRegistry
nodes.summary: Creates the plugin tool registry with add, remove, and lookup operations for namespace-qualified tool definitions contributed by plugins.

nodes.name: plugin-worker-manager.ts
nodes.summary: Manages the lifecycle of plugin worker processes, handling spawning, health monitoring, IPC message routing, crash recovery, and graceful shutdown.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Node.js child_process with structured IPC for worker communication and implements exponential backoff for crash recovery.

nodes.name: createPluginWorkerHandle
nodes.summary: Creates a handle for a single plugin worker process, managing its spawn, IPC communication, health checks, and restart logic on failure.

nodes.name: createPluginWorkerManager
nodes.summary: Creates the top-level worker manager that orchestrates multiple plugin worker handles, managing the pool of active workers per plugin.
